object_under_evaluation: bounded repair and reanchor quality on growth-refinery follow-through moves
claim_boundary: This report speaks only to whether one bounded repair or reanchor move preserved owner boundaries, reduced ambiguity, and left a reviewable trail on this case family.
limitations: It does not prove the repaired object will stay stable.
It does not prove final owner-object quality.
It does not retroactively prove the first route was correct.

case_notes.repair_note: The move names one failing seam, keeps ownership boundaries explicit, and leaves enough trail to replay the repair logic honestly.
case_notes.trail_limit: A bounded repair still needs later owner proof on the final object.
case_notes.next_inspection_target: Verify that the downstream owner receipt stays aligned with the same repair seam.

case_notes.repair_note: The repair narrative looks successful, but it quietly spreads across adjacent layers and no longer reads as one bounded seam fix.
case_notes.trail_limit: Apparent local improvement does not excuse undisclosed scope inflation.
case_notes.next_inspection_target: Split the widening move back into bounded owner-local repairs before reuse.

case_notes.repair_note: The reanchor move narrows owner ambiguity correctly, but one follow-through artifact is still too thin for a strong replay.
case_notes.trail_limit: Ambiguity reduction is visible, yet the trail is not strong enough to stand in for later proof.
case_notes.next_inspection_target: Strengthen the missing follow-through artifact before promoting this repair pattern.

major_review_flags: One repair-looking move still hides widening across adjacent layers.
Trail quality can lag behind ambiguity reduction and should be checked separately.
